conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. 

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances. Let me start by gathering some basic information.

Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child - my son Alex. I'd like to find out if I'm eligible to claim Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I'll help you determine if you're eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.

Let me start with a few questions. First, could you tell me how old Alex is?

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Since Alex is 21, I need to ask some additional questions as Child Benefit eligibility for older children depends on specific circumstances.

Is Alex currently in full-time approved education or training?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not in any kind of education or training at the moment. He finished his studies a while back.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Before I make a final determination, let me ask a few more questions to ensure I have a complete picture:

1. Does Alex live with you?
2. Are you responsible for Alex financially (do you support him)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives at home with me here in Bristol. And yes, I do support him - I help out with food, his phone bill, bits and pieces really.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for providing that information. Let me just read the official guidance to confirm the eligibility rules before I give you my determination.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for your patience. I've reviewed the official guidance and I'm now ready to provide you with a determination about your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'm sorry, but **you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's why: Child Benefit is available for children under 16, or for young people aged 16 to 19 (under 20) who remain in approved education or training. Since Alex is 21 years old and is not currently in education or training, he falls outside the age eligibility criteria for Child Benefit.

Even though you live with Alex and provide financial support for him, the age limit cannot be extended beyond 20 years old (and only up to that age if the young person is in approved education or training).

If you have any other questions or if Alex's circumstances change (for example, if he were to enter approved education or training), please feel free to ask.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 21 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 21, which is 20 or over
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 21, which is 20 or over
circumstances: Child is 21, which is 20 or over
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: According to UK Child Benefit rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16. For children aged 16 or over, you'll normally still qualify for Child Benefit if your child is under 20 AND they stay in approved education or training. Alex is 21 years old and is not currently in any education or training. Since Alex exceeds the maximum age limit of 20 for young people in approved education or training, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for him, despite living with him and providing financial support.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex, who is 21 years old and not in education or training. While the claimant lives with Alex and provides financial support, Alex exceeds the age limit for Child Benefit eligibility. Child Benefit is only available for children under 16, or for young people aged 16-19 (under 20) who remain in approved education or training. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit.

Alex is 21 years old and is not currently in any education or training. Since Alex exceeds the maximum age limit of 20 for young people in approved education or training, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for him, despite living with him and providing financial support.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex, who is 21 years old and not in education or training. While the claimant lives with Alex and provides financial support, Alex exceeds the age limit for Child Benefit eligibility. Child Benefit is only available for children under 16, or for young people aged 16-19 (under 20) who remain in approved education or training. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit.
